banner.preRelease: We're pre-1.0 — building in the open. Help shape v1 →

assistant.title: How can we help?
assistant.subtitle: Support questions or commercial interest — we read everything.

commercial.lede: Evaluating the Enterprise or Cloud edition? Tell us a bit about your team and we'll reply within one business day.
commercial.name: Name
commercial.messagePlaceholder: We're evaluating molesignal Enterprise for ~50 services…
commercial.successBody: We'll reply to your email within one business day.
commercial.rateLimited: Too many messages from this network. Please try again in a bit.
commercial.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

support.communityNote: Community support is free for everyone running the open-source edition.

hero.title: What you'd build if you had time.
hero.subtitle: Logs, metrics, traces — one storage, one engine, self-hosted. OpenTelemetry-native.

home.demoTitle: Three signals. One trace_id. Five seconds.
home.demoBody: A trace, its logs, and the host metric for the same minute — natively correlated at the data plane, not stitched in a dashboard.
home.whyTitle: vs the two paths everyone else takes
home.whyBody: Commercial SaaS is correlated but expensive. OSS stacks are free but fragmented. molesignal is correlated and self-hosted.
home.archTitle: One storage. One engine. One bill.
home.archBody: Parquet on object storage + DataFusion + Arrow. Three signals share the data plane.
home.startTitle: Five minutes to your first query
home.startBody: One command. Postgres, MinIO, and molesignal standalone.
home.statsBody: Every commit lands publicly. Every issue is searchable. Pre-1.0, every voice still moves the roadmap.
home.designPartnerBody: We're recruiting 5–15 mid-size teams to help shape v1. Weekly founders chat. Real influence on the product. Free.

why.metaDescription: How molesignal compares to Datadog, New Relic, Splunk, and OSS stacks like Loki + Mimir + Tempo. With a cost calculator.
why.title: Why molesignal
why.lede: The three observability paths, and why we built a fourth.
why.pathsTitle: Three paths, each with a tax
why.saasBody: Datadog, New Relic, Splunk. Three signals are correlated, but the bill grows linearly. Mid-size teams pay $2k–10k/mo for 100 GB/day. Cut ingest and you lose visibility.
why.ossBody: Loki + Mimir + Tempo + Grafana. Free, but logs / metrics / traces live in three stores with three query languages. The trace ↔ log ↔ host metric jump is manual.
why.moleBody: One storage layer (Parquet on object store), one query engine (DataFusion + Arrow), one metadata layer (Postgres). Three signals correlated at the data plane. Self-hosted.
why.compareCaption: Every claim is testable. Spin up the sandbox, ingest your own data, and reproduce.
why.costBody: Pick your real-world ingest volume and retention. We compute Datadog at public pricing and molesignal as S3 + a small compute baseline.
why.notBody: We're a telemetry platform — not APM auto-instrumentation, not browser RUM, not a SaaS in v1, and not magical.

3am.title: 3am, page just fired
3am.body: SRE jumps three tabs to stitch a trace_id across logs, metrics, and traces. molesignal makes that one query.

budget.title: Budget cut by 30%
budget.body: CFO wants observability spend down. SaaS forces you to drop ingest; molesignal lets you keep visibility, change the cloud bill.

es-ck.title: Stuck maintaining ES/CK clusters
es-ck.body: Self-built scales operationally as fast as it scales data. molesignal is one set of components on Parquet + object storage.

multi-team.title: Multiple teams, one platform
multi-team.body: Planner-level org-rewrite means you don't deploy per-team. One process, many tenants, AI-grade isolation.

realtime.title: Alert latency matters
realtime.body: Prometheus eval-interval ≥ scrape-interval. With kind: realtime alerts, molesignal pages under a second.

architecture.metaDescription: How molesignal keeps three signals in one columnar layer, queries them with a vectorized engine, and stays multi-tenant from the planner down.
architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.lede: One storage layer. One query engine. One metadata layer.
architecture.dataPathBody: Tap a node to see what it does.
architecture.tapOrHover: Tap or hover a node to see what it does.
architecture.storageBody: Logs, metrics, and traces all land in one columnar format on object storage. The columnar layout makes cross-signal filters and time-range prunes cheap.
architecture.queryBody: A vectorized query engine plans SQL, PromQL, and VRL against the columnar store. The planner does multi-tenant org-rewrite, so tenant isolation lives at the query layer rather than at the deployment layer.
architecture.ingestBody: Ten supported on day one: OTLP HTTP, OTLP gRPC, Vector, Fluent Bit, Prometheus remote_write, Loki push, Splunk HEC, Jaeger, Zipkin, Syslog.
architecture.realtimeBody: Alerts with kind: realtime evaluate inline against the write path. Under 1 second from event to page.
architecture.multiTenantBody: Tenants are rewritten at planner time. One process serves many orgs without prefix isolation hacks.
architecture.perfBody: Benchmarks land with v1.0. Methodology: TSBS for metrics, custom log/trace harness against 1 TB/day. Track the work in the Roadmap.
architecture.perfBannerText: Benchmark methodology is published, numbers land with v1.0.
architecture.openBody: We're still figuring out cardinality limit policies for high-dimensional labels, and Parquet rotation under spiky ingest. If you're stuck on either, become a Design Partner.

ingest.label: Ingest
ingest.tooltip: Ten ingest protocols. OTLP HTTP/gRPC are first-class; Vector, Fluent Bit, Prometheus remote_write, Loki push, Splunk HEC, Jaeger, Zipkin, and Syslog all land on the same write path.

wal.label: WAL
wal.tooltip: A write-ahead log durably accepts every write before it rolls up to columnar storage — no data loss on a crash. A metadata store tracks orgs, streams, partitions, and schema.

storage.label: Storage
storage.tooltip: All three signals (logs, metrics, traces) share one columnar format on object storage. Time-partitioned for cheap prune-and-scan queries.

engine.label: Query Engine
engine.tooltip: A vectorized engine plans SQL / PromQL / VRL directly against columnar storage. The planner does multi-tenant org-rewrite, so tenant isolation lives at the query layer.

api.label: Query API
api.tooltip: Single HTTP/gRPC surface for product UI, dashboards, alerts, and third-party tools. Same engine, three signal queries side by side.

start.metaDescription: Five minutes from zero to your first query. docker compose, Helm chart, or binary install — pick your path.
start.title: Quick Start
start.lede: Five minutes to your first query.
start.prereqHelm: Kubernetes 1.27+ and Helm 3.x with read/write S3 credentials
start.prereqBinary: Linux x86_64 + writable workdir + 4 GB RAM
start.ingestBody: An OTLP HTTP POST. Any OpenTelemetry SDK, Vector, or Fluent Bit will produce the same shape.
start.queryBody: SQL against the stream. PromQL and VRL also work — same engine, same Parquet.
start.v1Notice: Helm charts and prebuilt binaries ship with v1.0 and aren't published yet. Use the Docker Compose path above to run molesignal today.

port.a: Set MOLE_HTTP_PORT in your env or change the host port mapping in docker-compose.yaml.

org.q: I get 'no such org' on the first query
org.a: The standalone profile creates a default org on first login. Visit http://localhost:5080 first and complete the bootstrap.

minio.q: MinIO admin says I can't write to the bucket
minio.a: Default creds are minioadmin/minioadmin. Check the bucket was created — molesignal creates it on first ingest, so send a single OTLP request first.

cargo.q: Cargo build fails when running from source
cargo.a: Rust 1.74+ required. Use rustup update stable. If you're on Apple Silicon, install Xcode CLT first.

alerts.a: kind: realtime requires an alert source on a write path stream. Check the alert is bound to the same stream you're writing to.

designPartner.metaDescription: Pre-1.0 recruitment — 5 to 15 mid-size teams help shape v1, with weekly founders chat and direct product influence.
designPartner.title: Become a Design Partner
designPartner.lede: We're rebuilding observability. Be early. Be heard.
designPartner.youGet1: Early access to release candidates and design docs
designPartner.youGet1Body: See the work before it ships. Comment, request changes, push back.
designPartner.youGet2Body: 30 min a week. Show us what hurts. We rework the roadmap when you push.
designPartner.youGet3Body: Pre-1.0, design partner asks tend to land. The Roadmap reflects this.
designPartner.youGet4Body: Only if you want it. Anonymity preserved by default.
designPartner.whoBody: Teams running 50 GB+/day on Datadog or self-built today; willing to spend 30 min a week with us. We're not chasing logos — fit beats prestige.
designPartner.after1: Reply within 48 hours from founders@molesignal.io with a scheduling link.
designPartner.after2: 30-min discovery call: what hurts, what you're using now, what would change your decision.
designPartner.after3: Onboarding doc + early-access access tokens + Discord invite.
designPartner.faq1A: Yes. We pay for compute and your time costs you nothing.
designPartner.faq2Q: How long am I expected to commit?
designPartner.faq2A: Targets are 30 min/week for 3 months, then re-evaluate. Cancel anytime.
designPartner.faq3Q: Can we fork or contribute upstream?
designPartner.faq3A: Yes. The project is Apache 2.0–licensed. Bring your engineers; we'll help merge.
designPartner.faq4Q: Do we need to sign an NDA?
designPartner.faq4A: No. We share work-in-progress publicly. If you need it for internal review, we can sign a mutual NDA.
designPartner.faq5Q: What if we change our mind?
designPartner.faq5A: Email us; you're out. No process, no friction.

form.biggestPainHint: Up to 400 characters. What hurts most today? We read every one.
form.submit: Apply to be a Design Partner →
form.footerNote: No spam. We reply to every application within 48 hours.
form.successTitle: Thank you. We'll reply within 48 hours.
form.successBody: Reply lands in your inbox from founders@molesignal.io with a 30-min discovery call scheduling link and our onboarding doc.
form.errorToast: Submission failed — please retry, or email founders@molesignal.io directly.
form.rateLimit: Too many submissions from your network right now. Give it a minute and try again — your details weren't lost.
form.validationEmail: Looks like a typo — please use a real email

cloud.metaDescription: molesignal Cloud is on the way. Self-hosted now, hosted later. Drop your email to get notified.
cloud.title: molesignal Cloud — waitlist
cloud.lede: Self-hosted now. Hosted later. Get notified.
cloud.consider1Body: molesignal control plane in our environment; storage and compute in yours. Best balance of ops simplicity and data sovereignty.
cloud.consider2Body: Fully hosted. We run everything. You point your collectors and pay per GB.
cloud.consider3Body: SOC2 Type II on the roadmap; HIPAA evaluation post-1.0.
cloud.whenBody: No date yet. We're focused on shipping v1.0 self-hosted first.

form.successBody: We'll email you the moment Cloud opens. No spam in between.
form.rateLimit: You're sending these a bit fast. Give it a minute and try again.

roadmap.metaDescription: What we're working on, what's next, and what's done. Every item links to its GitHub issue.
roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.lede: Building in the open. Every item links to GitHub.
roadmap.influenceBody: Open a GitHub issue, or apply to be a Design Partner. Pre-1.0, the loudest evidence wins.
roadmap.emptyPhase: Nothing in this phase yet. Check the others.

pricing.metaDescription: Three ways to run molesignal — Open Source (self-hosted, free), Enterprise (self-hosted with vendor support), and Cloud (fully managed, waitlist).
pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.lede: Three ways to run molesignal. Same engine. Different operating models.

oss.headline: Self-host on your own infra
oss.bestFor: SRE teams comfortable running their own observability platform.

enterprise.headline: Self-host with us on the line
enterprise.bestFor: Mid-to-large organisations that need SLAs, security patches, and a person to call.

cloud.headline: Point your collectors, pay per GB
cloud.bestFor: Teams that don't want to run infra. Bring-your-own-cloud and managed multi-tenant in evaluation.

features.compliance: SOC2 Type II on the roadmap; HIPAA post-1.0

free.q: Is the Open Source tier really free forever?
free.a: Yes. Apache 2.0 means you can run it, modify it, and ship it commercially without paying us. We make money on Enterprise and Cloud.

oss-vs-cloud.q: What's the difference between Open Source and Cloud feature-wise?
oss-vs-cloud.a: Same engine, same ingest, same query languages. The Cloud tier adds the operational layer — we run the database, ship updates, watch the alerts. You only own your collector config.

enterprise-when.q: When do I need Enterprise vs Open Source?
enterprise-when.a: When losing observability for an afternoon is more expensive than a year of Enterprise — typically when you cross ~100 GB/day or have audit obligations. Talk to us; we'll be honest if Open Source is enough.

cloud-launch.a: No public date yet. We're focused on shipping v1.0 self-hosted first. Waitlist signups get the first invites.

migrate.q: Can I migrate from Open Source to Cloud later?
migrate.a: Yes. Same Parquet schema and same query engine — your dashboards and queries port over without rewrites.

open-core.q: Will a feature I rely on get moved behind a paywall later?
open-core.a: No. Our open-core promise is explicit: the Apache-2.0 core stays fully usable for a single team, forever. We only charge for buyer-side features like SSO, audit logs, and SLA support — never core observability.

cloud-free.q: Will Cloud have a free tier?
cloud-free.a: Yes — a free tier is planned for launch, so you can try managed molesignal without a contract. Cloud isn't live yet; join the waitlist for first access.

pricing.stewardshipNote: Worried a feature you depend on will get paywalled later?
pricing.ctaBody: If you're running observability for fun, learning, or under ~100 GB/day, Open Source is enough. If you need SLAs or compliance support, talk to us. If you want zero ops, get on the Cloud waitlist.

blog.metaDescription: Engineering posts from the molesignal team — architecture deep-dives, post-mortems, and notes from building in the open.
blog.title: Blog
blog.lede: Engineering posts from the team building molesignal.
blog.noPostsBody: We're writing the first launch article. Come back, or subscribe via the Cloud waitlist.

errors.notFoundBody: The URL doesn't match anything we ship. The site is small — these links cover most paths:
errors.errorBody: We've been notified. If this keeps happening, please file an issue with the URL.

legal.disclaimer: This is a plain-language summary, not formal legal advice. Have a question? Email <email>founders@molesignal.io</email>.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: molesignal is an open-source project. This site collects only the minimum data needed to run a waitlist and follow up with design partners — nothing more. This page describes exactly what we collect, who processes it, and how to have it removed.

sections.heading: Information you give us
body: We only store what you choose to submit through our forms:
bullets: Design Partner application (/design-partner): your name, email, company size, current observability stack, and (optionally) your biggest pain point.
Each form includes a hidden anti-spam field. If it is filled in, the submission is silently discarded and nothing is stored.

sections.heading: How your submission is handled
body: When you submit a form, the details above — together with your IP address and a timestamp — are sent as a notification email to our founding team and kept in that mailbox so we can follow up. Your email address is set as the reply-to address.
We also write a short server log entry for each submission (for the waitlist, your email address; for a Design Partner application, your email address, company size, and current stack).

sections.heading: IP addresses and abuse prevention
body: We count requests per IP address to rate-limit our form endpoints and prevent abuse. This is short-lived and is not used to build a profile of you. As noted above, the IP address of a successful submission is also included in the notification email to our team.

sections.heading: Analytics (Plausible)
body: When analytics is enabled, we use Plausible — a privacy-friendly analytics tool that uses no cookies, does not store IP addresses, does not track you across sites, and respects the Do-Not-Track signal. It records only aggregate page views and a few defined conversion events, and those events contain no personal information. Analytics loads only when an analytics domain is configured for the site.

sections.heading: Browser local storage
body: Your theme preference and whether you have dismissed the pre-release banner are stored locally in your browser and are never sent to our servers. This is a convenience setting, not tracking.

sections.heading: Third parties we use
body: We share data only with the providers needed to deliver the above, and we never sell your personal data.
bullets: Resend — our email provider. It transmits the notification email described above. We do not add you to any marketing list or audience.
Hosting and infrastructure — the site runs on serverless hosting; request IP addresses pass through our rate-limiting layer.

sections.heading: How we use your data
body: We use your information only for the purpose for which you submitted it: to notify you when Cloud opens, to follow up on a Design Partner application, and for necessary product-update communication. We do not use it beyond that purpose.

sections.heading: Retention and your rights
body: We keep your data only as long as needed for that purpose — waitlist entries until after we notify you that the product is ready, applications until after the evaluation is complete. You can ask us to access, correct, or delete your data at any time.

privacy.contact: To exercise any of these rights, or for any privacy question, email <email>founders@molesignal.io</email>.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms cover your use of the molesignal project website. molesignal is an open-source project, and this site is its marketing and documentation home.

sections.heading: Open source and "as is"
body: The molesignal software is open source. Content on this site — including cost comparisons and other figures — is provided on an "as is" basis, may change at any time, and is for general reference only. It is not a commercial commitment or a guarantee of any particular result.

sections.heading: License
body: The molesignal software is released under the Apache License 2.0. Website content and copy are © 2026 molesignal contributors.

sections.heading: Acceptable use
body: Please do not abuse the site or its form endpoints. Automated scraping, spam, and attempts to overwhelm our forms are not permitted, and requests are rate-limited to protect the service.

sections.heading: No warranty and limitation of liability
body: The site and its content are provided without warranties of any kind, to the extent permitted by law. We are not liable for any loss arising from your use of the site or your reliance on its content.

sections.heading: Changes to these terms
body: We may update these terms from time to time. The "last updated" date at the top of this page shows when they last changed; continued use of the site means you accept the current version.

terms.license: The full license text is available at <license>apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0</license>.

crossSignalDemo.heading: One trace_id, three views.
crossSignalDemo.footerSuffix: across all three. No copy-paste between tools.

rows.sameStorageDetail: Whether logs, metrics, traces share one storage layer.
rows.correlationDetail: Jump from a trace to its logs to the host metric — without copy/paste.

costCalculator.disclaimerPrefix: Order-of-magnitude estimates based on Datadog's public list pricing as of
costCalculator.disclaimerBody: . Datadog: logs ingest + 15-day indexed events (assuming all ingested logs are indexed — an upper bound) + APM spans, with a retention multiplier. molesignal: S3 Standard storage + baseline Postgres + compute scaling per 100 GB/day. Your mileage will vary with redundancy, region, commit discounts, and workload shape.

github.title: Star on GitHub
github.body: Show support and stay in the loop. The repo is the project's source of truth and the fastest way to file an issue.

discord.title: Discord — launching soon
discord.body: A small space for design partners and contributors to chat. Drop your email at /design-partner and we'll send an invite.
discord.bodyLive: Chat with design partners and contributors. Click to join our community space.
discord.soonTitle: Launching soon — drop your email at /design-partner for an invite

enterprise.metaDescription: Self-host molesignal with a support SLA, backported security patches, and a team on the line. Built for organisations with audit and uptime obligations.
enterprise.title: Self-host molesignal, with us on the line
enterprise.lede: Run the open-source engine on your own infrastructure — with a support SLA, security patches, and a direct line to the people who build it.
enterprise.getLede: Everything in Open Source, plus the commitments an organisation needs to depend on it.

get.slaBody: A contractual response time, not community best-effort. Email plus a private channel.
get.patchesBody: Critical fixes backported to your pinned version, so you don't have to chase main to stay safe.
get.auditBody: We help you answer security questionnaires and review your deployment against your reliability targets.
get.onboardingBody: Hands-on setup and operator training so your team runs it confidently from day one.
get.supportBody: A private line to the maintainers — not a ticket queue.
get.priorityBody: Your requirements move up the roadmap. You help shape what v1 and beyond look like.

enterprise.securityBody: Apache-2.0 means the full source is auditable, and self-hosting means your telemetry never leaves your infrastructure. SOC2 Type II is on the roadmap.
enterprise.roadmapBody: These are the controls the person signing the check usually needs. They're planned, not shipped — tell us which ones you need and we'll prioritise.
enterprise.deployBody: Enterprise is self-hosted: deploy anywhere you run Docker or Kubernetes and keep all data in your own environment. We're on the line for support — we never need access to your telemetry.
enterprise.ctaBody: We're pre-1.0 and building in the open with a small group of design partners. If you have audit or uptime obligations, tell us what you need — we'll be honest about what's ready today and what's on the way.

security.metaDescription: How molesignal handles vulnerability disclosure, source transparency, and your data. Apache-2.0, self-hosted, SOC2 on the roadmap.
security.title: Security & trust
security.lede: How we handle vulnerability disclosure, source transparency, and your data — stated plainly, pre-1.0 and all.
security.disclosureBody: Found something? Email security@molesignal.io or open a private GitHub Security Advisory. We acknowledge reports quickly and credit reporters who want it.
security.openBody: The full source is Apache-2.0 and auditable — no closed core to take on faith. Self-hosted means your telemetry never leaves your own infrastructure; we never need access to it.
security.dataBody: Self-host: data stays entirely in your environment. Cloud (in development): bring-your-own-cloud keeps storage and compute in your account, or fully managed multi-tenant — you choose.
security.complianceBody: We are pre-1.0 and not yet certified. We won't claim a badge we don't hold.

compliance.soc2: SOC2 Type II — on the roadmap
compliance.gdpr: Self-hosting keeps you in control of data residency today

security.requestBody: Sending us a vendor security questionnaire, or need an NDA before a deeper review? Reach out — we'll tell you what we can share today and what's coming.

stewardship.metaDescription: What stays free and open forever, and what we charge for. molesignal's open-core stewardship commitment, Apache-2.0.
stewardship.title: Our open-core promise
stewardship.lede: The honest version of what stays free forever and what we charge for — so you can depend on the open-source core without fear.
stewardship.promiseBody: The Apache-2.0 core stays fully usable for a single team, forever. We will not move core observability features behind a paywall. If you can run it today, you can run it tomorrow.
stewardship.freeLede: The whole engine — everything a team needs to actually run observability.
stewardship.paidLede: The features the person who signs the check needs — not the person who uses the tool every day. Charging here is what keeps the core free.
stewardship.codeBody: As we add Enterprise features, they live in a clearly separated module under a commercial license — the Apache-2.0 core stays whole and auditable. You always know which line you're on.
